verses.latin_vulgate: Quis est iste, qui venit de Edom,  tinctis vestibus de Bosra ?  iste formosus in stola sua,  gradiens in multitudine fortitudinis suæ ?  Ego qui loquor justitiam,  et propugnator sum ad salvandum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִי־זֶ֣ה ׀ בָּ֣א מֵאֱד֗וֹם חֲמ֤וּץ בְּגָדִים֙ מִבׇּצְרָ֔ה זֶ֚ה הָד֣וּר בִּלְבוּשׁ֔וֹ צֹעֶ֖ה בְּרֹ֣ב כֹּח֑וֹ אֲנִ֛י מְדַבֵּ֥ר בִּצְדָקָ֖ה רַ֥ב לְהוֹשִֽׁיעַ׃
verses.king_james: Who is this that cometh from Edom, with dyed garments from Bozrah? this that is glorious in his apparel, travelling in the greatness of his strength? I that speak in righteousness, mighty to save.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare ergo rubrum est indumentum tuum,  et vestimenta tua sicut calcantium in torculari ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַדּ֥וּעַ אָדֹ֖ם לִלְבוּשֶׁ֑ךָ וּבְגָדֶ֖יךָ כְּדֹרֵ֥ךְ בְּגַֽת׃
verses.king_james: Wherefore art thou red in thine apparel, and thy garments like him that treadeth in the winefat?

verses.latin_vulgate: Torcular calcavi solus,  et de gentibus non est vir mecum ;  calcavi eos in furore meo,  et conculcavi eos in ira mea :  et aspersus est sanguis eorum super vestimenta mea,  et omnia indumenta mea inquinavi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פּוּרָ֣ה ׀ דָּרַ֣כְתִּי לְבַדִּ֗י וּמֵֽעַמִּים֙ אֵֽין־אִ֣ישׁ אִתִּ֔י וְאֶדְרְכֵ֣ם בְּאַפִּ֔י וְאֶרְמְסֵ֖ם בַּחֲמָתִ֑י וְיֵ֤ז נִצְחָם֙ עַל־בְּגָדַ֔י וְכׇל־מַלְבּוּשַׁ֖י אֶגְאָֽלְתִּי׃
verses.king_james: I have trodden the winepress alone; and of the people there was none with me: for I will tread them in mine anger, and trample them in my fury; and their blood shall be sprinkled upon my garments, and I will stain all my raiment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dies enim ultionis in corde meo ;  annus redemptionis meæ venit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֛י י֥וֹם נָקָ֖ם בְּלִבִּ֑י וּשְׁנַ֥ת גְּאוּלַ֖י בָּֽאָה׃
verses.king_james: For the day of vengeance is in mine heart, and the year of my redeemed is come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Circumspexi, et non erat auxiliator ;  quæsivi, et non fuit qui adjuvaret :  et salvavit mihi brachium meum,  et indignatio mea ipsa auxiliata est mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַבִּיט֙ וְאֵ֣ין עֹזֵ֔ר וְאֶשְׁתּוֹמֵ֖ם וְאֵ֣ין סוֹמֵ֑ךְ וַתּ֤וֹשַֽׁע־לִי֙ זְרֹעִ֔י וַחֲמָתִ֖י הִ֥יא סְמָכָֽתְנִי׃
verses.king_james: And I looked, and there was none to help; and I wondered that there was none to uphold: therefore mine own arm brought salvation unto me; and my fury, it upheld me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et conculcavi populos in furore meo,  et inebriavi eos in indignatione mea,  et detraxi in terram virtutem eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָב֤וּס עַמִּים֙ בְּאַפִּ֔י וַאֲשַׁכְּרֵ֖ם בַּחֲמָתִ֑י וְאוֹרִ֥יד לָאָ֖רֶץ נִצְחָֽם׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: And I will tread down the people in mine anger, and make them drunk in my fury, and I will bring down their strength to the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Miserationum Domini recordabor ;  laudem Domini  super omnibus quæ reddidit nobis Dominus,  et super multitudinem bonorum domui Israël,  quæ largitus est eis secundum indulgentiam suam,  et secundum multitudinem misericordiarum suarum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חַֽסְדֵ֨י יְהֹוָ֤ה ׀ אַזְכִּיר֙ תְּהִלֹּ֣ת יְהֹוָ֔ה כְּעַ֕ל כֹּ֥ל אֲשֶׁר־גְּמָלָ֖נוּ יְהֹוָ֑ה וְרַב־טוּב֙ לְבֵ֣ית יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אֲשֶׁר־גְּמָלָ֥ם כְּֽרַחֲמָ֖יו וּכְרֹ֥ב חֲסָדָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: I will mention the lovingkindnesses of the Lord, and the praises of the Lord, according to all that the Lord hath bestowed on us, and the great goodness toward the house of Israel, which he hath bestowed on them according to his mercies, and according to the multitude of his lovingkindnesses.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit : Verumtamen populus meus est,  filii non negantes ;  et factus est eis salvator.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ אַךְ־עַמִּ֣י הֵ֔מָּה בָּנִ֖ים לֹ֣א יְשַׁקֵּ֑רוּ וַיְהִ֥י לָהֶ֖ם לְמוֹשִֽׁיעַ׃
verses.king_james: For he said, Surely they are my people, children that will not lie: so he was their Saviour.

verses.latin_vulgate: In omni tribulatione eorum non est tribulatus,  et angelus faciei ejus salvavit eos :  in dilectione sua et in indulgentia sua  ipse redemit eos,  et portavit eos, et elevavit eos  cunctis diebus sæculi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּֽכׇל־צָרָתָ֣ם ׀ (לא) [ל֣וֹ] צָ֗ר וּמַלְאַ֤ךְ פָּנָיו֙ הוֹשִׁיעָ֔ם בְּאַהֲבָת֥וֹ וּבְחֶמְלָת֖וֹ ה֣וּא גְאָלָ֑ם וַֽיְנַטְּלֵ֥ם וַֽיְנַשְּׂאֵ֖ם כׇּל־יְמֵ֥י עוֹלָֽם׃
verses.king_james: In all their affliction he was afflicted, and the angel of his presence saved them: in his love and in his pity he redeemed them; and he bare them, and carried them all the days of old.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipsi autem ad iracundiam provocaverunt,  et afflixerunt spiritum Sancti ejus :  et conversus est eis in inimicum,  et ipse debellavit eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהֵ֛מָּה מָר֥וּ וְעִצְּב֖וּ אֶת־ר֣וּחַ קׇדְשׁ֑וֹ וַיֵּהָפֵ֥ךְ לָהֶ֛ם לְאוֹיֵ֖ב ה֥וּא נִלְחַם־בָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: But they rebelled, and vexed his holy Spirit: therefore he was turned to be their enemy, and he fought against them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et recordatus est dierum sæculi Moysi, et populi sui.  Ubi est qui eduxit eos de mari  cum pastoribus gregis sui ?  Ubi est qui posuit in medio ejus  spiritum Sancti sui ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּזְכֹּ֥ר יְמֵֽי־עוֹלָ֖ם מֹשֶׁ֣ה עַמּ֑וֹ אַיֵּ֣ה ׀ הַֽמַּעֲלֵ֣ם מִיָּ֗ם אֵ֚ת רֹעֵ֣י צֹאנ֔וֹ אַיֵּ֛ה הַשָּׂ֥ם בְּקִרְבּ֖וֹ אֶת־ר֥וּחַ קׇדְשֽׁוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Then he remembered the days of old, Moses, and his people, saying, Where is he that brought them up out of the sea with the shepherd of his flock? where is he that put his holy Spirit within him?

verses.latin_vulgate: qui eduxit ad dexteram Moysen,  brachio majestatis suæ ;  qui scidit aquas ante eos,  ut faceret sibi nomen sempiternum ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מוֹלִיךְ֙ לִימִ֣ין מֹשֶׁ֔ה זְר֖וֹעַ תִּפְאַרְתּ֑וֹ בּ֤וֹקֵֽעַ מַ֙יִם֙ מִפְּנֵיהֶ֔ם לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת ל֖וֹ שֵׁ֥ם עוֹלָֽם׃
verses.king_james: That led them by the right hand of Moses with his glorious arm, dividing the water before them, to make himself an everlasting name?

verses.latin_vulgate: qui eduxit eos per abyssos,  quasi equum in deserto  non impingentem ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מוֹלִיכָ֖ם בַּתְּהֹמ֑וֹת כַּסּ֥וּס בַּמִּדְבָּ֖ר לֹ֥א יִכָּשֵֽׁלוּ׃
verses.king_james: That led them through the deep, as an horse in the wilderness, that they should not stumble?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quasi animal in campo descendens,  spiritus Domini ductor ejus fuit.  Sic adduxisti populum tuum,  ut faceres tibi nomen gloriæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּבְּהֵמָה֙ בַּבִּקְעָ֣ה תֵרֵ֔ד ר֥וּחַ יְהֹוָ֖ה תְּנִיחֶ֑נּוּ כֵּ֚ן נִהַ֣גְתָּ עַמְּךָ֔ לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת לְךָ֖ שֵׁ֥ם תִּפְאָֽרֶת׃
verses.king_james: As a beast goeth down into the valley, the Spirit of the Lord caused him to rest: so didst thou lead thy people, to make thyself a glorious name.

verses.latin_vulgate: Attende de cælo, et vide  de habitaculo sancto tuo, et gloriæ tuæ.  Ubi est zelus tuus, et fortitudo tua,  multitudo viscerum tuorum et miserationum tuarum ?  Super me continuerunt se.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַבֵּ֤ט מִשָּׁמַ֙יִם֙ וּרְאֵ֔ה מִזְּבֻ֥ל קׇדְשְׁךָ֖ וְתִפְאַרְתֶּ֑ךָ אַיֵּ֤ה קִנְאָֽתְךָ֙ וּגְב֣וּרֹתֶ֔ךָ הֲמ֥וֹן מֵעֶ֛יךָ וְֽרַחֲמֶ֖יךָ אֵלַ֥י הִתְאַפָּֽקוּ׃
verses.king_james: Look down from heaven, and behold from the habitation of thy holiness and of thy glory: where is thy zeal and thy strength, the sounding of thy bowels and of thy mercies toward me? are they restrained?

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu enim pater noster :  et Abraham nescivit nos,  et Israël ignoravit nos :  tu, Domine, pater noster,  redemptor noster, a sæculo nomen tuum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־אַתָּ֣ה אָבִ֔ינוּ כִּ֤י אַבְרָהָם֙ לֹ֣א יְדָעָ֔נוּ וְיִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל לֹ֣א יַכִּירָ֑נוּ אַתָּ֤ה יְהֹוָה֙ אָבִ֔ינוּ גֹּאֲלֵ֥נוּ מֵעוֹלָ֖ם שְׁמֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: Doubtless thou art our father, though Abraham be ignorant of us, and Israel acknowledge us not: thou, O Lord, art our father, our redeemer; thy name is from everlasting.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare errare nos fecisti, Domine, de viis tuis ;  indurasti cor nostrum ne timeremus te ?  Convertere propter servos tuos,  tribus hæreditatis tuæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָ֣מָּה תַתְעֵ֤נוּ יְהֹוָה֙ מִדְּרָכֶ֔יךָ תַּקְשִׁ֥יחַ לִבֵּ֖נוּ מִיִּרְאָתֶ֑ךָ שׁ֚וּב לְמַ֣עַן עֲבָדֶ֔יךָ שִׁבְטֵ֖י נַחֲלָתֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: O Lord, why hast thou made us to err from thy ways, and hardened our heart from thy fear? Return for thy servants’ sake, the tribes of thine inheritance.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quasi nihilum possederunt populum sanctum tuum :  hostes nostri conculcaverunt sanctificationem tuam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לַמִּצְעָ֕ר יָרְשׁ֖וּ עַם־קׇדְשֶׁ֑ךָ צָרֵ֕ינוּ בּוֹסְס֖וּ מִקְדָּשֶֽׁךָ׃
verses.king_james: The people of thy holiness have possessed it but a little while: our adversaries have trodden down thy sanctuary.

verses.latin_vulgate: Facti sumus quasi in principio, cum non dominareris nostri,  neque invocaretur nomen tuum super nos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָיִ֗ינוּ מֵֽעוֹלָם֙ לֹא־מָשַׁ֣לְתָּ בָּ֔ם לֹֽא־נִקְרָ֥א שִׁמְךָ֖ עֲלֵיהֶ֑ם לֽוּא־קָרַ֤עְתָּ שָׁמַ֙יִם֙ יָרַ֔דְתָּ מִפָּנֶ֖יךָ הָרִ֥ים נָזֹֽלּוּ׃
verses.king_james: We are thine: thou never barest rule over them; they were not called by thy name.
